User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the DocuParse Pro application, linking to an external authentication system and storing user-specific preferences and activities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and display purposes. (Not for authentication)

displayName.description: A human-readable name for the user, suitable for display.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

starredStrategyIds.description: References to ExtractionStrategy entities that this user has starred. (Relationship: User N:N ExtractionStrategy)

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded technical document, its metadata, and processing status within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

name.description: The original file name of the uploaded document.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this document. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

uploadDateTime.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

fileType.description: The file type or extension of the document (e.g., 'PDF', 'DOCX', 'TXT').

storagePath.description: The URI or internal path where the document content is stored.

status.description: The current processing status of the document (e.g., 'UPLOADED', 'PROCESSING', 'PROCESSED', 'FAILED').

processedDataId.description: Reference to the ExtractedData entity generated from this document, if processed. (Relationship: Document 1:1 ExtractedData)

extractionStrategyId.description: Reference to the ExtractionStrategy used to process this document, if any. (Relationship: ExtractionStrategy 1:N Document)

ExtractionStrategy.title: ExtractionStrategy
ExtractionStrategy.description: Defines a reusable collection of extraction rules for parsing technical documents. These strategies can be published to a marketplace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExtractionStrategy entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the extraction strategy.

description.description: A detailed description explaining what the strategy does and what kind of documents it's designed for.

authorId.description: Reference to the User who created or published this strategy. (Relationship: User 1:N ExtractionStrategy)

isPublic.description: Indicates whether this strategy is public and available in the marketplace.

ruleIds.description: References to ExtractionRule entities that compose this strategy. (Relationship: ExtractionStrategy N:N ExtractionRule)

starCount.description: The total number of times this strategy has been starred by users.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the extraction strategy was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the extraction strategy was last updated.

ExtractionRule.title: ExtractionRule
ExtractionRule.description: Defines a single, specific rule or pattern for extracting data from documents. Rules can be composed into strategies.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExtractionRule entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the extraction rule.

description.description: A detailed explanation of what this rule extracts and how.

configuration.description: A JSON string or formatted text containing the specific parameters for the rule (e.g., regex pattern, key-value pair, AI prompt).

authorId.description: Reference to the User who created this extraction rule. (Relationship: User 1:N ExtractionRule)

strategyIds.description: References to ExtractionStrategy entities that include this rule. (Relationship: ExtractionRule N:N ExtractionStrategy)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the extraction rule was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the extraction rule was last updated.

ExtractedData.title: ExtractedData
ExtractedData.description: Stores the structured information extracted from a document using a specific extraction strategy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExtractedData entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which this data was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 ExtractedData)

extractionStrategyId.description: Reference to the ExtractionStrategy that was used to produce this extracted data. (Relationship: ExtractionStrategy 1:N ExtractedData)

extractedContent.description: The extracted structured data, typically stored as a JSON string.

extractedDateTime.description: Timestamp when the data extraction process was completed.

isValidated.description: Indicates whether the extracted data has been reviewed and validated by a user.

validatedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who performed the validation, if any. (Relationship: User 1:N ExtractedData)

validationDateTime.description: Timestamp when the data was last validated, if applicable.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner of the profile. Includes denormalized 'starredStrategyIds' for efficient lookup of user-starred strategies.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores technical documents uploaded by a specific user. This collection ensures that each user's historical files are stored in isolation. Authorization is dependent on `resource.data.userId` matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the uploaded document.

definition.description: Stores structured data extracted from documents. To ensure authorization independence, each document in this collection includes a denormalized 'userId' field, matching the path's wildcard, derived from its associated Document entity. This allows direct authorization checks against `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom this extracted data belongs.

params.name: extractedDataId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extracted data record.

definition.description: Contains custom extraction rules defined by a specific user. Authorization is dependent on `resource.data.authorId` matching `request.auth.uid`, ensuring privacy and ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created this extraction rule.

params.name: ruleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extraction rule.

definition.description: Hosts all extraction strategies, forming a central marketplace. Documents in this collection are secured by their `isPublic` status (for read access by any authenticated user) and `authorId` (for write access by the creator). Includes denormalized `starCount` for public display.

params.name: strategyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extraction strategy.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for DocuParse Pro is designed with a strong emphasis on authorization independence, clear data segregation, and simplified security rules. Following the core design principles, user-specific data is isolated under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, ensuring private and easily manageable access control. Public data, such as the extraction strategy marketplace, resides in its own top-level collection, with authorization logic handled directly on the document's attributes rather than through complex hierarchical dependencies.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) and Denormalization:**

1.  **User-owned Documents (`/users/{userId}/documents`):** Each user's uploaded documents are stored in a subcollection under their respective user ID. The `Document` entity already contains a `userId` field, which is explicitly checked against `request.auth.uid` in security rules. This eliminates the need for `get()` operations to check ownership, allowing atomic creation and updates.

2.  **User-owned Extracted Data (`/users/{userId}/extractedData`):** Similar to documents, extracted data is also isolated per user. To achieve authorization independence as mandated, the `ExtractedData` entity, though not explicitly having a `userId` in the provided schema, **must be denormalized** to include a `userId` (or `ownerUserId`) field. This `userId` will be copied from the associated `Document` during creation. This denormalized field allows security rules to directly verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to perform a `get()` call to the parent `Document` or infer ownership from the path, thereby supporting atomic operations and simplified rule logic.

3.  **User-owned Extraction Rules (`/users/{userId}/extractionRules`):** Each user's custom extraction rules are stored in a subcollection. The `ExtractionRule` entity includes an `authorId` field, which directly maps to the `userId` in the path, ensuring easy and independent authorization checks.

4.  **Extraction Strategy Marketplace (`/extractionStrategies`):** All extraction strategies (public and private drafts) are stored in a single top-level collection. The `ExtractionStrategy` entity contains `authorId` and `isPublic` fields. Authorization rules will check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.authorId` for write access by the author, and `resource.data.isPublic == true` for read access by any authenticated user for marketplace browsing. No `get()` calls are needed as all necessary authorization context is denormalized directly within the strategy document.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) Support:**

*   **User-specific lists:** For `/users/{userId}/documents`, `/users/{userId}/extractedData`, and `/users/{userId}/extractionRules`, `list` operations are implicitly secured. A client requesting a list from `/users/myUserId/documents` must have `request.auth.uid == 'myUserId'`. The path itself acts as a strong filter, and rules only need to confirm the authenticated user matches the path's wildcard. This ensures that only a user's own data is returned, fulfilling the QAP principle.

*   **Extraction Strategy Marketplace:** For `/extractionStrategies`, a `list` operation will be allowed with specific conditions. Rules will permit reads where `resource.data.isPublic == true` (for marketplace browsing) or `resource.data.authorId == request.auth.uid` (for viewing/managing own strategies). Client-side queries will include `where('isPublic', '==', true)` to retrieve marketplace items, and the server-side rules will validate this filter, ensuring the integrity of the data returned and adhering to QAP.

This structure ensures that security rules are straightforward, explicit, and do not rely on costly and complex `get()` operations or break atomic transactions. Data integrity for ownership, timestamps, and denormalized `starCount` is maintained through consistent field usage and transactional updates for cross-document consistency where necessary (e.g., starring a strategy).
